summary.intent: Add a regression test for GitHub issue #798 (double-slash bug on Slack commands /new and /clear) and fix the underlying defect.
summary.outcome: Fix applied in dispatch.rs to defensively strip leading '/' from commands before matching; regression tests added in both dispatch.rs and webhook.rs; all tests pass and code is formatted and clippy-clean.

repo: Slack API sends command field with a leading slash (e.g. '/new'), while Telegram strips it with trim_start_matches('/') before calling dispatch_command, and Discord command names have no slash at all.

dispatch_command lives in crates/gateway/src/channel_events/commands/dispatch.rs and is the single convergence point for all channel command dispatch — fixing it there makes the gateway defensive regardless of caller behavior.

The ChannelEventSink trait (crates/channels/src/plugin.rs) has default implementations for most methods, so mocks only need to implement emit, dispatch_to_chat, dispatch_command, and request_disable_account.
The worktree path for this project is /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/quaint-accelerator.

code.finding: Passes event.command raw (with leading '/') to dispatch_command — bug source, now covered by gateway-level fix.
Same raw slash-pass bug as socket.rs; test added here using a mock ChannelEventSink to verify slash is stripped.
Telegram strips the leading '/' with trim_start_matches('/') before dispatch — the correct pattern that Slack paths were missing.
Fix location: strip leading '/' from command string before matching. Also where the unit test for slash-stripping was added.

workflow: When gateway state is too heavy for a unit test, a mock ChannelEventSink in the channel crate's test module is a practical alternative.
cargo fmt with nightly was required (not stable) for formatting.
Compilation check was run before tests to catch issues early.

friction: Setting up a meaningful test for dispatch_command required significant exploration because it needs heavy gateway state — led to multiple alternative approaches before settling on a mock EventSink in webhook.rs.
The assistant made an erroneous reference to SavedChannelFile as a trait method and had to correct the mock implementation with a follow-up edit.
Multiple rounds of reading webhook.rs were needed due to the file being long and the test structure requiring careful placement.
open_items: socket.rs has no equivalent command-dispatch test; coverage relies entirely on the gateway-level fix and the webhook.rs mock test.
The existing dispatch.rs test only checks registry consistency, not actual command routing — broader dispatch integration tests could be added.
